commands.review_summary:
The PR imports the existing update progress controller into `maybeOfferUpdateBeforeDoctor` and passes its callbacks to `runGatewayUpdate` with a stdout TTY guard and cleanup in `finally`.

PR surface: Source +8. Total +8 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, though I did not run the interactive updater: current main calls `runGatewayUpdate` from `openclaw doctor` without progress callbacks after printing one static update note.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair is needed; the next blocker is contributor-provided real behavior proof or maintainer live verification before merge.

target.title: fix(doctor): show per-step progress spinners during update
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw doctor` offers to update before running checks, but the update (fetch/rebase/build/ui:build/doctor) can take several minutes with **zero progress indication** — just a static "Running update (fetch/rebase/build/ui:build/doctor)…" message and then silence. It looks frozen.
- **Why it matters:** Users can't tell if the update is progressing or hung, leading to premature cancellation or confusion.
- **What changed:** Wired the existing `createUpdateProgress()` spinner system (already used by `openclaw update`) into the doctor update path. Each step now shows a spinner while running and a checkmark with duration on completion. Also guards spinner on `process.stdout.isTTY` and ensures cleanup via `try/finally`.
- **What did NOT change:** No changes to the update runner, progress system, or any other command. Only `src/commands/doctor-update.ts` was touched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: N/A (observed during normal usage)

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Before: static note "Running update (fetch/rebase/build/ui:build/doctor)…" with no further output until completion.

After: per-step spinners with labels and durations:
```
◐ Fetching latest changes
✓ Fetching latest changes (2.1s)
◐ Rebasing onto target commit
✓ Rebasing onto target commit (0.3s)
◐ Building
✓ Building (47.2s)
◐ Building UI assets
✓ Building UI assets (12.8s)
◐ Running doctor checks
✓ Running doctor checks (3.4s)
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Darwin 25.3.0)
- Runtime: Node 22.22.1
- Model/provider: N/A (CLI UX change)
- Integration/channel: N/A

### Steps

1. Run `openclaw doctor` from a git checkout
2. Answer "Yes" to "Update OpenClaw from git before running doctor?"
3. Observe terminal output during update

### Expected

Per-step spinner indicators showing progress for each update step (fetch, rebase, build, ui:build, doctor).

### Actual

Per-step spinners display correctly with step labels and durations.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets
- Manual test: ran `openclaw doctor` with local branch 4 commits behind `origin/main`, confirmed spinners for all steps (fetch, rebase, build, ui:build, doctor) with correct labels and durations.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: ran `openclaw doctor` update interactively with real commits to fetch/rebase, confirmed spinners show and complete for all steps
- Edge cases checked: non-TTY stdout (spinners disabled via `Boolean(process.stdout.isTTY)`); error during update (spinner cleaned up via `try/finally`)
- What I did **not** verify: npm/pnpm (non-git) update path (not affected by this change)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert `src/commands/doctor-update.ts` to remove `createUpdateProgress` usage
- Files/config to restore: `src/commands/doctor-update.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: spinner not stopping on error (mitigated by try/finally), ANSI escape sequences in piped/redirected output (mitigated by TTY check)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: spinner left running if `runGatewayUpdate` throws
  - Mitigation: `stop()` is called in a `finally` block

---

AI-assisted (Claude Opus 4.6). Fully tested locally.
Codex review: ran `codex review --base origin/main` — no findings.
target.author: amersheeny
